title: Inventory WFP program data, national feeding reports, enrollment, and nutrition-outcome data sources for low-income countries
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates WFP school feeding survey data, national program reports, enrollment and attendance data, and nutrition-outcome surveys into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit sub-national data availability and meal-quality documentation.
inputs: WFP State of School Feeding report and annual survey documentation
National school feeding program reports for target countries
Enrollment and attendance data from education management information systems
Anthropometric and dietary-diversity survey data (DHS, MICS)
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, geographic grain, and documented limitations — particularly sub-national coverage granularity and meal-quality documentation.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering sub-national coverage, meal-quality data, and nutritional-outcome measurement.

title: Map sub-national school feeding coverage for at least two low-income countries
outcome: A sub-national school feeding coverage analysis for at least two low-income countries, documenting data sources, national-vs-sub-national granularity, and coverage-gap assessment.
inputs: WFP survey data and national program reports for target countries
School-level feeding program data where available
Administrative boundary data for district-level aggregation
Enrollment data from education management information systems
expected_artifact: validation.md update with coverage methodology and data-granularity documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms coverage data extraction, sub-national granularity assessment, and data-gap documentation for each target country.
done_condition: Coverage analysis includes methodology, sub-national results, data-granularity documentation, and replication status for at least two countries.

title: Analyze nutritional impact of school feeding using anthropometric and dietary-diversity data where available
outcome: A nutritional-impact analysis linking school feeding coverage to anthropometric and dietary-diversity outcomes, with explicit documentation of meal-quality variation, enrollment-vs-nutrition evidence distinction, and concurrent-intervention confounds.
inputs: anthropometric survey data (DHS, MICS) for target countries
meal-quality and nutritional-content data where available
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with impact methodology and meal-quality documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms impact-linkage methodology, meal-quality documentation, and enrollment-vs-nutrition distinction for each target country.
done_condition: Nutritional-impact analysis includes methodology, meal-quality variation documentation, enrollment-vs-nutrition distinction, and replication status.

title: Red-team coverage overstatement and nutritional-impact exaggeration risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how national coverage statistics could mask district-level gaps, how coverage without meal-quality data could overstate nutritional impact, and how enrollment effects could be conflated with nutrition effects.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every coverage claim has sub-national granularity documentation, every nutritional claim has meal-quality context, and every effectiveness claim distinguishes enrollment from nutrition effects. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every coverage and impact claim is either blocked, softened to data-limited language with explicit meal-quality and enrollment-vs-nutrition caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match education ministry and WFP program decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the coverage-impact analysis would change any real decision for education ministries, WFP country offices, or school feeding program managers without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for education ministry and WFP audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that national statistics mask district-level gaps, or that coverage without meal-quality data is treated as nutritional impact. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., WFP Ethiopia school feeding coordinator), named decision (e.g., program expansion targeting to uncovered districts), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for claiming nutritional impact without meal-quality data), and replicated field-reality review.
